channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Democrats channel their outrage over DOGE, Ukraine and more in marathon Senate session
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The budget resolution from Republicans was on the agenda in the Senate, but the late-night debate encompassed so much more.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UK foreign secretary questions Russia's 'appetite' for peace and challenges Lavrov at tense G20 meet
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — U.K. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said he saw no appetite for peace from Russia in Ukraine after listening to a speech by Russia's top diplomat at a tense Group of 20 meeting in South Africa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, MICHELLE GUMEDE and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Luigi Mangione's CEO murder case raises concerns activist jurors may ignore evidence
item.description: Legal experts say voir dire, or jury selection, could be the key to deciding the fate of suspected UnitedHealthcare CEO assassin Luigi Mangione.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netflix to invest $1 billion on Mexican films and series
item.description: Netflix has pledged $1 billion to develop and produce films and series in Mexico over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KTLA 5

item.title: The man accused of stabbing Salman Rushdie declines to take the stand as the defense rests
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — The New Jersey man on trial in the 2022 stabbing of author Salman Rushdie declined to testify in his defense Thursday as his lawyers rested their case without calling any witnesses.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at $4B in funding for California high-speed rail
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Trump administration is once again targeting California's controversial high-speed rail project, with federal transportation officials on Thursday announcing an investigation and possible withdrawal of about $4 billion in federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Owners of Abby's Bakery in Los Fresnos charged with harboring illegal migrants
item.description: According to the complaint, the apprehension of the individuals was the result of a worksite enforcement action.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Yañez, NewsNation

item.title: 53-car pileup leaves 1 dead in North Carolina, adding to hundreds of snow-related wrecks
item.description: It's a mess out there from the Sandhills to the eastern stretches of the CBS 17 viewing area, causing dozens of wrecks along major and secondary roads in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Officials investigating bomb threat at Kennedy Center, police say
item.description: The Kennedy Center was evacuated on Thursday after a bomb threat was reported at the venue, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drake suprises pregnant fan with VIP tickets and $30,000
item.description: Drake gifted one fan in a major way during a Sydney show on his Anita Max Win Tour in Australia on Wednesday night. A pregnant fan, identified as Tiana Henderson, was sitting in the pit holding a sign that read "I'm 20 weeks pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wendy's plans to add more AI ordering assistants to restaurants nationwide
item.description: Wendy’s plans to expand its “voice-enabled AI order taking" at up to 600 restaurants nationwide by the end of the year, CEO Kirk Tanner confirmed during a recent earnings call. The burger chain currently uses its AI voice assistant, which it began testing in 2023, in around 100 Wendy’s locations.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Woman injured after ice slides off roof of tractor-trailer
item.description: Dan DeCesare said his wife, who was in the passenger seat, was hit directly in the face and chest by the chunk of ice.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Israeli UFC fighter talks antisemitism at self-defense seminar as Hamas hostages mark 500 days
item.description: Israeli UFC fighter Natan Levy marks 500th day of Hamas holding hostages with seminar aimed at teaching Jews how to defend themselves from possible antisemitic attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida leaders warn deadly consequences of ‘senior assassin’ game
item.description: They are urging parents and guardians to discuss with their children the potential dangers of playing this game.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE dividend checks a possibility, Trump says
item.description: (KRON) -- On Wednesday, President Donald Trump signaled he was open to the possibility of sending out stimulus checks to Americans using funds cut from the federal budget by Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency. The idea was initially floated on social media by Azoria CEO James Fishback.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Trump Education pick Linda McMahon clears committee, heads to full Senate
item.description: Linda McMahon, President Trump’s pick for Education secretary, passed the Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions (HELP) Committee on Thursday morning, sending her confirmation to the full Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Lt. Gov. says Texas Lottery will end if mobile lottery apps aren't banned
item.description: "It is a hard line, couriers have to go," Texas Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick said on Wednesday. The statement comes on the heels of an $83.5 million Lotto Texas jackpot, the fifth-highest in the game's history, which was won through the app Jackpocket -- a DraftKings subsidiary.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Peck, NewsNation

item.title: Reputed migrant gang members busted in NYC drug, gun raid but likely to avoid prosecution
item.description: Two suspected members of the violent Tren de Aragua (TdA) gang were busted in a gun and drug raid in a Big Apple auto repair shop earlier this month – and have since been cut loose on lesser charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: New, cheaper iPhone 16e has 'best battery life ever,' Apple says
item.description: "iPhone 16e has the best battery life ever on a 6.1-inch iPhone, lasting up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE," Apple said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Cynthia Erivo to host 78th Annual Tony Awards
item.description: "Wicked" actress Cynthia Erivo is set to host the 78th Annual Tony Awards. “I am so proud and excited to take on this glorious honor,” said Erivo.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Mountain lion captured after brief chase in Camarillo neighborhood
item.description: A mountain lion was tranquilized and captured Wednesday afternoon after it led animal control officers on a chase in Camarillo.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USDA working to rescind terminations for fired officials working on bird flu efforts
item.description: The accidental firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jury finds A$AP Rocky not guilty in felony assault trial
item.description: A verdict has been reached in the felony assault trial of rapper A$AP Rocky: NOT GUILTY. Jurors announced they’d made a decision just hours after the deliberations began Tuesday morning in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dozens of police recruits nationwide have died while training in last decade: probe
item.description: At least 29 recruits died during basic training at law enforcement academies around the country in the last decade, an AP investigation found.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Leonard Peltier leaves prison after Biden commuted his sentence in the killing of two FBI agents
item.description: SUMTERVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Native American activist Leonard Peltier was released from a Florida prison on Tuesday, weeks after then-President Joe Biden angered law enforcement officials by commuting his life sentence to home confinement in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC voters would likely support Gov. Kathy Hochul removing Adams as mayor, AP VoteCast shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As New York Gov. Kathy Hochul weighs whether to remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office, AP VoteCast shows that many New York City voters in last year's election were already on board with the move.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Postmaster general to step down, asks for replacement
item.description: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy, a former prominent Trump fundraiser who was appointed during the president's first term, announced Tuesday he would be stepping down.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to 6 killings in multiple states ordered held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE data access: Sources
item.description: King's exit from the administration is one of several departures of high-ranking officials concerned about DOGE staffers' potential unlawful access to private taxpayer information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Schools around the US confront anxiety over Trump's actions on immigration
item.description: In Fresno, California, social media rumors about impending immigration raids at the city's schools left some parents panicking — even though the raids were all hoaxes.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month. Here's a look at each of the crashes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
